item.title: 🚀 2023 年你应该贡献的 7 个开源项目 ⭐️⭐️ | 🚀 7 open-source projects you should contribute to in 2023 ⭐️⭐️
item.description:
我喜欢看到开源领域如此多的创新。 我认为我应该分享一些提升库。

让我们来做吧🚀

对您的选项卡、桌面和任何应用程序进行无限录制
  • 使用公共链接与任何人共享录音
  • 在特定时间范围后删除或取消列出录音
  • 将现有视频上传并共享到 Snapify。

    • 以下是维护者所说的：

    在这里检查存储库：
    在本地管理密码的最简单方法。

    • 完全离线：无需互联网连接。
    • 加密备份和恢复
    • 使用设备凭证（PIN、图案、生物识别）进行身份验证
    • 自动备份

    以下是维护者所说的：

    在这里检查存储库：
    适用于 Go 的 SQL 查询生成器和 ORM/Factory 生成器，支持 PostgreSQL、MySQL 和 SQLite。

    • 查询生成器
    • 方便数据库查询的模型
    • 从数据库模式生成模型和工厂的代码

    以下是维护者所说的：

    在这里检查存储库：
    开源功能标志和 A/B 测试服务

    不涉及外部服务器，通过互联网发送标志数据没有延迟。 您的数据保留在您的网络内。

    以下是维护者所说的：

    在这里检查存储库：
    该平台通过为 OCI 容器提供持续交付和版本管理功能来实现这一目标。

    以下是维护者所说的：

    在这里检查存储库：
    以下是维护者所说的：

    在这里检查存储库：
    开源状态页面和警报系统。

    以下是维护者所说的：

    在这里检查存储库：
    您想出现在我的下一篇文章中吗？
    将您的库添加到： libraries.github20k.com

    一旦暂停，github20k 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，github20k将能够再次评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，github20k 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 github20k 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

    笔记：

    感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 github20k：

    使 github20k 的所有帖子不那么明显

    举报其他不当行为

    取消标记 github20k 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    I love seeing so much innovation in open-source. There are a few raising libraries I thought I should share.

    Let's do it 🚀

    1. Snapify

    Snapify allows you to record and share recordings asynchronously

    • Make unlimited recordings of your tab, desktop, and any application
    • Share recordings with anyone using a public link
    • Delete or un-list recordings after a specific timeframe
    • Upload and share existing videos to Snapify.

    Here is what the maintainer has to say:

    Check the repository here:
    github.com/MarconLP/snapify

    2. KeyPass

    The easiest way to manage you password locally.

    • Complete offline : no internet connection needed.
    • Encrypted Backup and Restore
    • Authenticate with device credentials (pin, pattern, biometrics)
    • Auto Backup

    Here is what the maintainer has to say:

    Check the repository here:
    github.com/yogeshpaliyal/KeyPass

    3. Bob

    SQL query builder and ORM/Factory generator for Go with support for PostgreSQL, MySQL and SQLite.

    • Query Builder
    • SQL Executor for convenient scanning of results
    • Models for convenient database queries
    • Code generation of Models and Factories from your database schema

    Here is what the maintainer has to say:

    Check the repository here:
    github.com/stephenafamo/bob

    4. SwitchFeat

    Open source feature flags and A/B testing service

    SwitchFeat is an open-source, self-hosted platform that makes it very easy to manage your feature flags and run A/B tests straight from your infrastructure. No external servers involved, no latency in sending flags data over the internet. Your data stays within your network.

    Here is what the maintainer has to say:

    Check the repository here:
    github.com/switchfeat-com/switchfeat

    5. Dyrector.io

    dyrector.io is a self-hosted container management platform.

    dyrector.io helps engineer & DevOps teams and individual developers to shift their focus from maintaining and setting up their containerized applications to developing their software. The platform achieves this by offering continuous delivery and version management capabilities to your OCI containers.

    Here is what the maintainer has to say:

    Check the repository here:
    github.com/dyrector-io/dyrectorio

    6. Hackertab.dev

    Hackertab makes it easy for you to stay up-to-date with the latest developer news, tools, jobs and events.

    • 🆕 Hourly updated content
    • 💻 Customizable by language or topic
    • 👍 Curated content from the best sources
    • 🔖 Bookmark and read it later
    • 🌙 Dark mode for when it gets late Even more features are going to come in the future!

    Here is what the maintainer has to say:

    Check the repository here:
    github.com/medyo/hackertab.dev

    7. Openstatus

    The Open-source Status Page and Alerting System.

    OpenStatus is an open source alternative to your current monitoring service with a beautiful status page.

    Here is what the maintainer has to say:

    Check the repository here:
    github.com/openstatusHQ/openstatus

    Do you want to be listed in my next article?
    Add your library to: libraries.github20k.com

    (it's free)

    Once suspended, github20k will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, github20k will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by github20k will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If github20k is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

    Note:

    Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag github20k:

    Make all posts by github20k less visible

    github20k consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

    Report other inappropriate conduct

    Unflagging github20k will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: nevodavid

item.title: 星期一模因 | Meme Monday
item.description:
一旦被暂停，Ben 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，本将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，ben 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 Ben 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 Ben：

使 Ben 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 Ben 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Once suspended, ben will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ben will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ben will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ben is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag ben:

Make all posts by ben less visible

ben consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging ben will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: ben

item.title: 我们如何构建一个 GPT 代码代理🤖，在 React 和 Node.js 中生成全栈 Web 应用程序，简单解释 | How we built a GPT code agent 🤖 that generates full-stack web apps in React & Node.js, explained simply
item.description:
我们创建了GPT Web App Generator ，它可以让您快速描述您想要创建的 Web 应用程序，并在几分钟内生成一个用 React、Node.js、Prisma 和 Wasp 编写的全栈代码库就在您面前，并且可以在本地下载和运行！

我们开始将此作为一个实验，看看我们如何使用 GPT 在我们正在开发的开源 JS Web 应用程序框架Wasp中生成全栈 Web 应用程序。 自推出以来，我们在短短几天内就生成了 3000 多个应用程序！

查看这篇博客文章，了解 GPT Web 应用程序生成器的实际应用，包括一分钟的演示视频、一些示例应用程序，并详细了解我们的未来计划。 或者，您可以在magic-app-generator.wasp-lang.dev/亲自尝试一下！

在这篇博文中，我们将探讨创建 GPT Web 应用程序生成器的技术方面：我们使用的技术、我们如何设计提示、我们遇到的挑战以及我们做出的选择！ 此外，生成器背后的所有代码都是开源的： 网络应用程序、 GPT 代码代理。

支持我们！ 如果您想表达对我们正在做的事情的支持，请考虑在 Github 上给我们一颗星！ 我们在 Wasp 所做的一切都是开源的，您的支持激励我们并帮助我们不断简化 Web 应用程序开发并减少样板代码。

效果如何🤔？

首先，让我们快速解释一下我们最终得到了什么以及它的表现如何。

生成器中的输入是应用程序名称、应用程序描述（自由格式文本）和几个简单的选项，例如主要应用程序颜色、温度、身份验证方法和要使用的 GPT 模型。

作为输出，Generator 会输出一个工作全栈 Web 应用程序的整个 JS 代码库：前端、后端和数据库。 前端是 React + Tailwind，后端是 NodeJS 和 Express，为了处理数据库，我们使用 Prisma。 这一切都与Wasp框架连接在一起。

您可以在此处查看生成的代码库的示例： magic-app-generator.wasp-lang.dev/resul ...。

对于更简单的应用程序，例如TodoApp或MyPlants ，它通常会生成没有错误的代码，并且您可以开箱即用地运行它们。

生成的 TodoApp 如下所示：

对于更复杂的应用程序，例如包含帖子和评论的博客，它仍然会生成合理的代码库，但有时会出现一些错误。 对于更复杂的应用程序，它通常不会完全跟进，而是在某种程度的复杂性上停止，并用 TODO 填充其余部分或省略功能，因此它有点像所要求的简化模型。 总体而言，它针对生成 CRUD 业务 Web 应用程序进行了优化。

这使得它成为一个很好的工具，可以用可靠的原型启动您的下一个 Web 应用程序项目，甚至可以动态生成可用的简单应用程序！

它是如何运作的⚙️？

当我们开始构建 Generator 时，我们给自己设定了以下目标：

  • 我们必须能够在几周内建造它
  • 将来必须相对容易维护
  • 它需要快速且廉价地生成应用程序（几分钟，< 1 美元）
  • 生成的应用程序应该有尽可能少的错误

因此，为了简单起见，我们不进行任何 LLM 级别的工程或微调，而是使用 OpenAI API（特别是 GPT3.5 和 GPT4）来生成应用程序的不同部分，同时为其提供正确的上下文每时每刻（文档片段、示例、指南……） 。 为了确保生成的应用程序的连贯性和质量，我们没有给代码代理太多的自由，而是通过生成应用程序一步一步地大力指导它。

作为第零步，我们在没有 GPT 的情况下，仅根据用户选择的选项（原色、身份验证方法）确定性地生成一些代码文件：其中包括项目的一些配置文件、一些基本的全局 CSS 和一些身份验证逻辑。 您可以在这里看到这个逻辑（我们称之为“骨架”文件）： Github 上的代码。

然后，代码代理就接管了！

代码代理分三个主要阶段进行工作：

  1. 规划📝
  2. 生成🏭
  3. 修复🔧

由于 GPT4 比 GPT3.5 慢得多，而且成本也高得多（每分钟令牌数量以及每分钟请求数量也有较低的速率限制），因此我们仅使用 GPT4 进行规划，因为这是关键的一步，之后我们就使用GPT3.5来完成剩下的工作。

至于每个应用程序的成本💸：当我们混合使用 GPT4 和 GPT3.5 时，一个应用程序通常消耗 25,000 到 60,000 个代币，每个应用程序的成本约为0.1 到 0.2 美元。 如果我们只使用 GPT4 运行它，那么成本是 10 倍，即从1 美元到 2 美元。

对话只是一个消息列表，其中每条消息都有内容和角色，其中角色指定谁“说”了该内容 → 是“用户”（您）还是“助理”（GPT）。

需要注意的重要一点是，没有状态/内存的概念：每个 API 调用都是完全独立的，GPT 唯一知道的是您当时提供给它的对话！

如果您想知道 ChatGPT（在后台使用 GPT 的网络应用程序）如何在没有内存的情况下工作→那么，每次您写一条消息时，到目前为止的整个对话都会再次重新发送！ 这里还有一些额外的智能机制在发挥作用，但这确实是其核心。

官方指南，官方API参考。

步骤＃1：规划📝

一旦给出应用程序描述和标题，代码代理首先生成一个计划：它是组成应用程序的实体、操作（查询和操作）和页面的列表。 有点像应用程序的初稿。 它还没有生成代码 → 相反，它会提供它们的名称和一些其他详细信息，包括它们应该如何表现的简短描述。

这是通过向 GPT 发出单个 API 请求来完成的，其中提示包含以下内容：

  • 有关 Wasp 框架的简短信息+ 一些 Wasp 代码的示例。
  • 我们通过描述其架构来解释我们想要生成 Plan ，解释它是什么以及它如何表示为JSON 。
  • 我们提供了Plan 的一些示例，以 JSON 表示。
  • 我们希望它遵循一些规则和指南（例如“计划应该至少有一页”，“确保生成一个用户实体”）。
  • 仅以有效 JSON 响应的形式返回计划，而不返回其他文本的说明。
  • 应用程序名称和描述（由用户提供）。

您可以在此处的代码中看到我们如何生成这样的提示。

另外，这里还有一个指向要点的链接，其中包含 TodoApp 提示的实际实例。

然后，GPT 使用 JSON 进行响应（希望如此），我们对其进行解析，然后我们就有了一个计划！ 我们将在以下步骤中使用此计划来推动应用程序其他部分的生成。 请注意，GPT 有时会向 JSON 响应添加文本或返回无效的 JSON，因此我们构建了一些简单的方法来克服这些问题，稍后我们将详细解释。

我们上面刚刚描述的用于生成计划的提示设计实际上与其他步骤非常相似（例如生成和修复步骤及其各自的子步骤），因此让我们介绍一下这些共同点。

我们使用的所有提示或多或少都遵循相同的基本结构：

  • 一般背景
    • 关于 Wasp 框架是什么的简短信息。
    • 关于我们现在正在生成的内容（例如 NodeJS 代码示例或 React 代码示例）的文档片段（如果需要，还带有代码示例）。
  • 项目上下文：我们在前面的步骤中生成的与当前步骤相关的内容。
  • 关于我们现在想要生成的内容的说明 + 其 JSON 模式 + 此类 JSON 响应的示例。
  • 规则和指南：这是一个警告它所犯的常见错误的好地方，或者给它一些额外的建议，并强调什么需要发生，什么不能发生。
  • 仅使用有效的 JSON 进行响应，而不使用其他文本的说明。
  • 原始用户提示：应用程序名称和描述（由用户提供）。

我们将原始用户提示符放在最后，因为这样我们可以在看到原始用户提示符的开始后在系统消息中告诉 GPT（我们有一个特殊的标头），它需要将其之后的所有内容视为应用程序描述而不是作为做什么的说明→这样我们试图防御潜在的提示注入。

步骤#2：生成🏭

生成计划后，生成器将逐步执行该计划，并要求 GPT 生成每个 Web 应用程序片段，同时为其提供文档、示例和指南。 每次生成 Web 应用程序片段时，生成器都会将其放入整个应用程序中。 这就是我们大部分工作的切入点：在正确的时刻为 GPT 配备正确的信息。

在我们的例子中，我们对计划中的所有操作（操作和查询：NodeJs 代码）以及计划中的所有页面（React 代码）执行此操作，每个操作都有一个提示。 因此，如果我们有 2 个查询、3 个操作和 2 个页面，则将是 2+3+2 = 7 个 GPT 提示/请求。 提示的设计如前所述。

生成操作时，我们向 GPT 提供有关先前生成的实体的信息，而在生成页面时，我们向 GPT 提供有关先前生成的实体和操作的信息。

步骤＃3：修复🔧

最后，生成器会尽力修复 GPT 之前可能引入的任何错误。 为了进一步增强这个过程，我们不仅要求它修复以前的代码，还向它提供要注意的事项的说明，例如我们注意到它经常犯的常见错误类型，并指出它我们能够自行发现的任何具体错误。

关于检测错误以报告给 GPT，理想情况下，您将进行完整的 REPL → 这意味着通过解释器/编译器运行生成的代码，然后将其发送进行修复，依此类推，直到所有问题都得到修复。

在我们的例子中，由于时间限制，通过 TypeScript 编译器运行整个项目对我们来说是不可行的，但我们使用了一些更简单的静态分析工具，例如 Wasp 编译器（用于 .wasp 文件）和 Prisma 模型的prisma format架构，并将其发送到 GPT 来修复它们。 我们还编写了一些我们自己的简单启发式方法，能够发现一些常见错误。

在提示中，我们通常会重复我们之前在生成步骤中提供的相同指南，同时还添加一些针对常见错误的额外指示，这通常会有所帮助，它修复了之前错过的内容。 但是，通常不是所有事情，而是有些事情仍然会通过。 有些事情我们就是无法让它一致地修复，例如，Wasp 特定的 JS 导入，无论我们多么强调它需要对它们做什么，它只会继续把它们弄乱。 在这种情况下，即使是 GPT4 也不是完美的。 对于这种情况，如果可能的话，我们最终编写了自己的启发式方法来修复这些错误（ 修复 JS 导入）。

我们尝试/学到的东西

解释💬

我们尝试告诉 GPT 解释它在修复错误时做了什么：它将修复哪些错误，以及它修复了哪些错误，因为我们读到这可以提供帮助，但我们没有看到其性能的明显改进。

测试🧪

测试代码代理的性能很困难。

在我们的例子中，我们的代码代理需要几分钟的时间才能生成新的应用程序，并且您需要直接使用 OpenAI API 运行测试。 此外，由于结果是不确定的，因此很难判断输出是否受到您所做的更改的影响。

最后，评估输出本身可能很困难（尤其是在我们的例子中，它是一个完整的全栈 Web 应用程序）。

理想情况下，我们会建立一个系统，在该系统中我们只能运行整个生成过程的一部分，并且我们可以为每个不同的参数集自动运行特定部分多次（其中包括不同的提示，但也包括参数）例如模型类型（gpt4 与 gpt3.5）、温度等），以便比较每个参数集的性能。

理想情况下，评估性能也应该是自动化的，例如我们会计算编译过程中的错误和/或评估应用程序设计的质量→但这也相当困难。

不幸的是，我们没有时间建立这样的系统，所以我们主要是手动进行测试，这是相当主观的并且容易受到随机性的影响，并且仅对具有相当大影响的更改有效，而你可以'并没有真正检测到那些小的优化。

背景与智慧🧠

当我们开始研究 Generator 时，我们认为 GPT 上下文的大小将是主要问题。 然而，我们最后没有遇到任何上下文问题 → 我们想要指定的大部分内容都适合 2k 到最多 4k 个令牌，而 GPT3.5 的上下文高达 16k！

相反，我们在它的“智能”方面遇到了更大的问题→ 这意味着 GPT 不会遵循我们明确告诉它要遵循的规则，或者会做我们明确禁止它做的事情。 事实证明，GPT4 比 GPT3.5 更能遵守规则，但即使是 GPT4 也会一遍又一遍地犯一些错误，并忘记特定的规则（即使有足够多的上下文）。 处理 JSON 作为响应 📋

正如本文前面提到的，在我们与 GPT 的所有交互中，我们总是要求它以 JSON 形式返回响应，为此我们指定架构并给出一些示例。

然而，GPT 仍然并不总是遵循该规则，有时会在 JSON 周围添加一些文本，或者在格式化 JSON 时出错。

我们处理这个问题的方法是通过两个简单的修复：

  1. 收到 JSON 后，我们将删除从开头到点击{的所有字符，以及从结尾到点击}的所有字符。 简单的启发式，但它在实践中非常适合删除 JSON 周围的冗余文本，因为 GPT 通常不会在该文本中包含任何{或} 。
  2. 如果我们无法解析 JSON，我们会再次将其发送到 GPT 进行修复。 我们包括之前的提示及其最后的答案（包含无效的 JSON），并添加修复它的说明 + 我们得到的 JSON 解析错误。 我们重复几次直到它正确（或者直到我们放弃）。

在实践中，这两种方法为我们处理了 99% 的无效 JSON 情况。

注意：在我们实现代码代理时，OpenAI 发布了 GPT 的新功能“函数”，这基本上是一种让 GPT 按照您描述的模式以结构化 JSON 进行响应的机制。 因此，用“函数”来做这件事可能更有意义，但我们已经让它运行良好，所以我们就坚持了下来。

处理服务中断🚧

我们直接调用 OpenAI API，因此我们很快注意到它经常会返回 503 - 服务不可用 - 特别是在高峰时段（例如欧洲中部时间下午 3 点）。

因此，建议采用某种重试机制，最好是指数退避，使您的代码代理对于服务中的此类随机中断以及潜在的速率限制来说是多余的。 我们采用了指数退避的重试机制，效果很好。

温度🌡️

温度决定了 GPT 的创造性，但它的创造性越大，它就越不“稳定”。 它产生更多幻觉，也更难遵守规则。
温度是 0 到 2 之间的数字，默认值为 1。

我们尝试了不同的值并发现以下结果：

  • 也就是说，我认为我们测试低于 0.7 的值还不够，我们当然可以在这方面做更多的工作。

    我们最终使用 0.7 作为默认值，除了进行修复的提示之外，对于那些我们使用较低值 0.5 的提示，因为看起来 GPT 在修复为 0.7 时改变了太多东西（太有创意了）。 我们的逻辑是：在编写第一个版本的代码时让它具有创造性，然后在修复它时让它更传统一些。 再说一次，我们还没有对所有这些进行足够的测试，所以这当然是我希望我们进行更多探索的事情。

    未来🔮

    虽然我们最终对在如此短的时间内构建的性能印象深刻，但我们也想尝试许多不同的想法来进一步改进它。 在这个发展如此迅速的生态系统中，还有许多途径有待探索，以至于很难达到让您觉得自己探索了所有选项并找到最佳解决方案的程度。

    一些未来值得尝试的想法：

    1. 我们对代码代理生成的代码设置了相当多的限制，以确保它足够好：我们不允许它创建帮助程序文件，不允许包含 npm 依赖项，没有 TypeScript，没有高级 Wasp 功能，……。 我们希望解除限制，从而允许创建更复杂、更强大的应用程序。

    2. 我们可以让用户在生成应用程序的第一个版本后与其进行交互，而不是让我们的代码代理一次完成所有操作：提供额外的提示，例如修复某些内容、向应用程序添加某些功能，做一些不同的事情，…… 这里最困难的事情是弄清楚在哪个时刻向 GPT 提供哪个上下文并适当地设计体验，但我确信这是可行的，并且它将把 Generator 的可用性提升到一个新的水平。
      另一种选择是允许在初始生成步骤之间进行干预→例如，在生成计划后，允许用户通过向 GPT 提供附加指令来调整它。

    3. 找到一个适合我们目的的开源法学硕士，并根据我们的目的对其进行微调/预训练。 如果我们可以教它更多关于 Wasp 和我们使用的技术的知识，那么我们就不必在每个提示中都包含它，我们可以节省相当多的上下文+让法学硕士更加关注我们在迅速的。 我们也可以自己托管它，并对成本和费率限制有更多的控制。

    4. 对代码代理采取不同的做法：让它更加自由。 我们可以不那么仔细地指导它，而是可以教它允许要求的所有不同的事情（要求文档、要求示例、要求生成应用程序的某个部分、要求查看某个已经生成的部分）应用程序，...）并让它更自由地引导自己。 它可以不断地生成计划、执行计划、更新计划等等，直到达到平衡状态。 这种方法可能会带来更大的灵活性，并且可能能够生成更复杂的应用程序，但它也需要更多的代币和强大的法学硕士来驱动它→我相信，随着法学硕士变得更有能力，这种方法将变得更加可行。

    支持我们！ 如果您想表达对我们正在做的事情的支持，请考虑在 Github 上给我们一颗星！ 我们在 Wasp 所做的一切都是开源的，您的支持激励我们并帮助我们不断简化 Web 应用程序开发并减少样板代码。

    另外，如果您对我们如何改进代码代理有任何想法，或者我们可以以某种方式帮助您 -> 请随时加入我们的Discord 服务器，让我们聊天！



原文:

We created GPT Web App Generator, which lets you shortly describe the web app you would like to create, and in a matter of minutes, a full-stack codebase, written in React, Node.js, Prisma, and Wasp, will be generated right in front of you, and available to download and run locally!

We started this as an experiment, to see how well we could use GPT to generate full-stack web apps in Wasp, the open-source JS web app framework that we are developing. Since we launched, we had more than 3000 apps generated in just a couple of days!

Check out this blog post to see GPT Web App Generator in action, including a one-minute demo video, few example apps, and learn a bit more about our plans for the future. Or, try it out yourself at magic-app-generator.wasp-lang.dev/ !

In this blog post, we are going to explore the technical side of creating the GPT Web App Generator: techniques we used, how we engineered our prompts, challenges we encountered, and choices we made! (Note from here on we will just refer to it as the “Generator”, or “code agent” when talking about the backend)

Also, all the code behind the Generator is open source: web app, GPT code agent.

Support us! 🙏⭐️

If you wish to express your support for what we are doing, consider giving us a star on Github! Everything we do at Wasp is open source, and your support motivates us and helps us to keep making web app development easier and with less boilerplate.

How well does it work 🤔?

First, let’s quickly explain what we ended up with and how it performs.

Input into our Generator is the app name, app description (free form text), and a couple of simple options such as primary app color, temperature, auth method, and GPT model to use.

As an output, Generator spits out the whole JS codebase of a working full-stack web app: frontend, backend, and database. Frontend is React + Tailwind, the backend is NodeJS with Express, and for working with the database we used Prisma. This is all connected together with the Wasp framework.

You can see an example of generated codebase here: magic-app-generator.wasp-lang.dev/resul… .

Generator does its best to produce code that works out of the box → you can download it to your machine and run it. For simpler apps, such as TodoApp or MyPlants, it often generates code with no mistakes, and you can run them out of the box.

Here is what a generated TodoApp looks like:

For a bit more complex apps, like a blog with posts and comments, it still generates a reasonable codebase but there are some mistakes to be expected here and there. For even more complex apps, it usually doesn’t follow up completely, but stops at some level of complexity and fills in the rest with TODOs or omits functionality, so it is kind of like a simplified model of what was asked for. Overall, it is optimized for producing CRUD business web apps.

This makes it a great tool for kick-starting your next web app project with a solid prototype, or to even generate working, simple apps on the fly!

How does it work ⚙️?

When we set out to build the Generator, we gave ourselves the following goals:

  • we must be able to build it in a couple of weeks
  • it has to be relatively easy to maintain in the future
  • it needs to generate the app quickly and cheaply (a couple of minutes, < $1)
  • generated apps should have as few mistakes as possible

Therefore, to keep it simple, we don’t do any LLM-level engineering or fine-tuning, instead, we just use OpenAI API (specifically GPT3.5 and GPT4) to generate different parts of the app while giving it the right context at every moment (pieces of docs, examples, guidelines, …). To ensure the coherence and quality of the generated app, we don’t give our code agent too much freedom but instead heavily guide it, step by step, through generating the app.

As step zero, we generate some code files deterministically, without GPT, just based on the options that the user chose (primary color, auth method): those include some config files for the project, some basic global CSS, and some auth logic. You can see this logic here (we call those “skeleton” files): code on Github .

Then, the code agent takes over!

The code agent does its work in 3 main phases:

  1. Planning 📝
  2. Generating 🏭
  3. Fixing 🔧

Since GPT4 is quite slower and significantly more expensive than GPT3.5 (also has a lower rate limit regarding the number of tokens per minute, and also the number of requests per minute), we use GPT4 only for the planning, since that is the crucial step, and then after that, we use GPT3.5 for the rest.

As for cost per app 💸: one app typically consumes from 25k to 60k tokens, which comes to about $0.1 to $0.2 per app, when we use a mix of GPT4 and GPT3.5. If we run it just with GPT4, then the cost is 10x, which is from $1 to $2.

🎶 Intermezzo: short explanation of OpenAI Chat Completions API

OpenAI API offers different services, but we used only one of them: “chat completions”.

API itself is actually very simple: you send over a conversation, and you get a response from the GPT.

The conversation is just a list of messages, where each message has content and a role, where the role specifies who “said” that content → was it “user” (you), or “assistant” (GPT).

The important thing to note is that there is no concept of state/memory: every API call is completely standalone, and the only thing that GPT knows about is the conversation you provide it with at that moment!

If you are wondering how ChatGPT (the web app that uses GPT in the background) works with no memory → well, each time you write a message, the whole conversation so far is resent again! There are some additional smart mechanisms in play here, but that is really it at its core.

Official guide, official API reference.

Step #1: Planning 📝

A Wasp app consists of Entities (Prisma data models), Operations (NodeJS Queries and Actions), and Pages (React).

Once given an app description and title, the code agent first generates a Plan: it is a list of Entities, Operations (Queries and Actions), and Pages that comprise the app. So kind of like an initial draft of the app. It doesn’t generate the code yet → instead, it comes up with their names and some other details, including a short description of what they should behave like.

This is done via a single API request toward GPT, where the prompt consists of the following:

  • Short info about the Wasp framework + an example of some Wasp code.
  • We explain that we want to generate the Plan, explain what it is, and how it is represented as JSON, by describing its schema.
  • We provide some examples of the Plan, represented as JSON.
  • Some rules and guidelines we want it to follow (e.g. “plan should have at least 1 page”, “make sure to generate a User entity”).
  • Instructions to return the Plan only as a valid JSON response, and no other text.
  • App name and description (as provided by the user).

You can see how we generate such a prompt in the code here.

Also, here is a link to a gist with an actual instance of this prompt for a TodoApp.

GPT then responds with a JSON (hopefully), that we parse, and we have ourselves a Plan! We will use this Plan in the following steps, to drive our generation of other parts of the app. Note that GPT sometimes adds text to the JSON response or returns invalid JSON, so we built in some simple approaches to overcome these issues, which we explain in detail later.

🎶 Intermezzo: Common prompt design

The prompt design we just described above for generating a Plan is actually very similar for other steps (e.g. the Generation and Fixing steps along with their respective sub-steps), so let’s cover those commonalities.

All of the prompts we use more or less adhere to the same basic structure:

  • General context
    • Short info about what Wasp framework is.
    • Doc snippets (with code examples if needed) about whatever we are generating right now (e.g. examples of NodeJS code, or examples of React code).
  • Project context: stuff we generated in the previous steps that is relevant to the current step.
  • Instructions on what we want to generate right now + JSON schema for it + example of such JSON response.
  • Rules and guidelines: this is a good place to warn it about common mistakes it makes, or give it some additional advice, and emphasize what needs to happen and what must not happen.
  • Instructions to respond only with a valid JSON, and no other text.
  • Original user prompt: app name and description (as provided by the user).

We put the original user prompt at the end because then we can tell GPT in the system message after it sees the start of the original user prompt (we have a special header for it), that it needs to treat everything after it as an app description and not as instructions on what to do → this way we attempt to defend from the potential prompt injection.

Step #2: Generating 🏭

After producing the Plan, Generator goes step by step through the Plan and asks GPT to generate each web app piece, while providing it with docs, examples, and guidelines. Each time a web app piece is generated, Generator fits it into the whole app. This is where most of our work comes in: equipping GPT with the right information at the right moment.

In our case, we do it for all the Operations in the Plan (Actions and Queries: NodeJs code), and also for all the Pages in the Plan (React code), with one prompt for each. So if we have 2 queries, 3 actions, and 2 pages, that will be 2+3+2 = 7 GPT prompts/requests. Prompts are designed as explained previously.

Code on Github: generating an Operation, generating a Page.

When generating Operations, we provide GPT with the info about the previously generated Entities, while when generating Pages, we provide GPT with the info about previously generated Entities and Operations.

Step #3: Fixing 🔧

Finally, the Generator tries its best to fix any mistakes that GPT might have introduced previously. GPT loves fixing stuff it previously generated → if you first ask it to generate some code, and then just tell it to fix it, it will often improve it!

To enhance this process further, we don’t just ask it to fix previous code, but also provide it with instructions on what to keep an eye out for, like common types of mistakes that we noticed it often does, and also point it to any specific mistakes we were able to detect on our own.

Regarding detecting mistakes to report to GPT, ideally, you would have a full REPL going on → that means running the generated code through an interpreter/compiler, then sending it for repairs, and so on until all is fixed.

In our case, running the whole project through the TypeScript compiler was not feasible for us with the time limits we put on ourselves, but we used some simpler static analysis tools like Wasp’s compiler (for the .wasp file) and prisma format for Prisma model schemas, and sent those to GPT to fix them. We also wrote some simple heuristics of our own that are able to detect some of the common mistakes.

Our code (& prompt) for fixing a Page.

Our code (& prompt) for fixing Operations.

In the prompt, we would usually repeat the same guidelines we provided previously in the Generation step, while also adding a couple of additional pointers to common mistakes, and that usually helps, it fixes stuff it missed before. But, often not everything, instead something will still get through. Some things we just couldn’t get it to fix consistently, for example, Wasp-specific JS imports, no matter how much we emphasized what it needed to do with them, it would just keep messing them up. Even GPT4 wasn’t perfect in this situation. For such situations, when possible, we ended up writing our own heuristics that would fix those mistakes (fixing JS imports).

Things we tried/learned

Explanations 💬

We tried telling GPT to explain what it did while fixing mistakes: which mistakes it will fix, and which mistakes it fixed, since we read that that can help, but we didn’t see visible improvement in its performance.

Testing 🧪

Testing the performance of your code agent is hard.

In our case, it takes a couple of minutes for our code agent to generate a new app, and you need to run tests directly with the OpenAI API. Also, since results are non-deterministic, it can be pretty hard to say if output was affected by the changes you did or not.

Finally, evaluating the output itself can be hard (especially in our case when it is a whole full-stack web app).

Ideally, we would have set up a system where we can run only parts of the whole generation process, and we could automatically run a specific part a number of times for each of different sets of parameters (which would include different prompts, but also parameters like type of model (gpt4 vs gpt3.5), temperature and similar), in order to compare performance for each of those parameter sets.

Evaluation performance would also ideally be automated, e.g. we would count the mistakes during compilation and/or evaluate the quality of app design → but this is also quite hard.

We, unfortunately, didn’t have time to set up such a system, so we were mostly doing testing manually, which is quite subjective and vulnerable to randomness, and is effective only for changes that have quite a big impact, while you can’t really detect those that are minor optimizations.

Context vs smarts 🧠

When we started working on the Generator, we thought the size of GPT’s context would be the main issue. However, we didn’t have any issues with context at the end → most of what we wanted to specify would fit into 2k to max 4k tokens, while GPT3.5 has context up to 16k!

Instead, we had bigger problems with its “smarts” → meaning that GPT would not follow the rules we very explicitly told it to follow, or would do things we explicitly forbid it from doing. GPT4 proved to be better at following rules than GPT3.5, but even GPT4 would keep doing some mistakes over and over and forgetting about specific rules (even though there was more than enough context). The “fixing” step did help with this: we would repeat the rules there and GPT would pick up more of them, but often still not all of them.

Handling JSON as a response 📋

As mentioned earlier in this article, in all our interactions with GPT, we always ask it to return the response as JSON, for which we specify the schema and give some examples.

However, GPT still doesn’t always follow that rule, and will sometimes add some text around the JSON, or will make a mistake in formatting JSON.

The way we handled this is with two simple fixes:

  1. Upon receiving JSON, we would remove all the characters from the start until we hit {, and also all chars from the end until we hit }. Simple heuristic, but it works very well for removing redundant text around the JSON in practice since GPT will normally not have any { or } in that text.
  2. If we fail to parse JSON, we send it again for repairs, to GPT. We include the previous prompt and its last answer (that contains invalid JSON) and add instructions to fix it + JSON parse errors we got. We repeat this a couple of times until it gets it right (or until we give up).

In practice, these two methods took care of invalid JSON in 99% of the cases for us.

NOTE: While we were implementing our code agent, OpenAI released new functionality for GPT, “functions”, which is basically a mechanism to have GPT respond with a structured JSON, following the schema of your description. So it would likely make more sense to do this with “functions”, but we already had this working well so we just stuck with it.

Handling interruptions in the service 🚧

We were calling OpenAI API directly, so we noticed quickly that often it would return 503 - service unavailable - especially during peak hours (e.g. 3 pm CET).

Therefore, it is recommended to have some kind of retry mechanism, ideally with exponential backoff, that makes your code agent redundant to such random interruptions in the service, and also to potential rate limiting. We went with the retry mechanism with exponential backoff and it worked great.

Temperature 🌡️

Temperature determines how creative GPT is, but the more creative it gets, the less “stable” it is. It hallucinates more and also has a harder time following rules.
A temperature is a number from 0 to 2, with a default value of 1.

We experimented with different values and found the following:

  • ≥ 1.5 would every so and so start giving quite silly results with random strings in it.
  • ≥ 1.0, < 1.5 was okish but was introducing a bit too many mistakes.
  • ≥ 0.7, < 1.0 was optimal → creative enough, while still not having many mistakes.
  • ≤ 0.7 seemed to perform similarly to a bit higher values, but with a bit less creativity maybe.

That said, I don’t think we tested values below 0.7 enough, and that is something we could certainly work on more.

We ended up using 0.7 as our default value, except for prompts that do fixing, for those we used a lower value of 0.5 because it seemed like GPT was changing stuff too much while fixing at 0.7 (being too creative). Our logic was: let it be creative when writing the first version of the code, then have it be a bit more conventional while fixing it. Again, we haven’t tested all this enough, so this is certainly something I would like us to explore more.

Future 🔮

While we ended up being impressed with the performance of what we managed to build in such a short time, we were also left wanting to try so many different ideas on how to improve it further. There are many avenues left to be explored in this ecosystem that is developing so rapidly, that it is hard to reach the point where you feel like you explored all the options and found the optimal solution.

Some of the ideas that would be exciting to try in the future:

  1. We put quite a few limitations regarding the code that our code agent generates, to make sure it works well enough: we don’t allow it to create helper files, to include npm dependencies, no TypeScript, no advanced Wasp features, … . We would love to lift the limitations, therefore allowing the creation of more complex and powerful apps.

  2. Instead of our code agent doing everything in one shot, we could allow the user to interact with it after the first version of the app is generated: to provide additional prompts, for example, to fix something, to add some feature to the app, to do something differently, …. The hardest thing here would be figuring out which context to provide to the GPT at which moment and designing the experience appropriately, but I am certain it is doable, and it would take the Generator to the next level of usability.
    Another option is to allow intervention in between initial generation steps → for example, after the plan is generated, to allow the user to adjust it by providing additional instructions to the GPT.

  3. Find an open-source LLM that fits the purpose and fine-tune / pre-train it for our purpose. If we could teach it more about Wasp and the technologies we use, so we don’t have to include it in every prompt, we could save quite some context + have the LLM be more focused on the rules and guidelines we are specifying in the prompt. We could also host it ourselves and have more control over the costs and rate limits.

  4. Take a different approach to the code agent: let it be more free. Instead of guiding it so carefully, we could teach it about all the different things it is allowed to ask for (ask for docs, ask for examples, ask to generate a certain piece of the app, ask to see a certain already generated piece of the app, …) and would let it guide itself more freely. It could constantly generate a plan, execute it, update the plan, and so on until it reaches the state of equilibrium. This approach potentially promises more flexibility and would likely be able to generate apps of greater complexity, but it also requires quite more tokens and a powerful LLM to drive it → I believe this approach will become more feasible as LLMs become more capable.

Support us! ⭐️

If you wish to express your support for what we are doing, consider giving us a star on Github! Everything we do at Wasp is open source, and your support motivates us and helps us to keep making web app development easier and with less boilerplate.

Also, if you have any ideas on how we could improve our code agent, or maybe we can help you somehow -> feel free to join our Discord server and let's chat!
item.author: martinsos

item.title: 过去一周的 7 个精选 DEV 帖子 | Top 7 Featured DEV Posts from the Past Week
item.description:
每个星期二，我们都会根据流量、参与度和编辑策划的提示汇总前一周的热门帖子。 通常的一周从星期一开始，到星期日结束，但不用担心，我们会考虑本周晚些时候发布的帖子。

这种列表永远不会真正完整，因此请在这篇精彩文章的评论中添加您最喜欢的 DEV 作者！

您将学习如何为图像创建清晰的描述，探索仅使用键盘的导航世界等等，以便每个人都可以像专业人士一样浏览您的网站！

请不要编写令人困惑的条件

在这里，我们将与@somedood讨论我们的代码中如何体现令人困惑的条件。 在此过程中，我们还探索了不同的重构技术来提高条件控制流的可读性。

空 Git 提交的魔力

尽管看似违反直觉，但空提交可以为文档提供宝贵的好处，从而触发自动化。 真实 DOM、虚拟 DOM、影子 DOM，有什么区别？

您可能很难在真实 DOM、虚拟 DOM 和影子 DOM 之间保持清醒的头脑，以及它们如何协同工作以创建干净、高性能的文档对象模型。 让我们和@lyndsiwilliams一起深入了解吧！

在这里，我们将与@joanayebola一起探索 CSS 网格的关键概念，并通过实际代码示例演示如何使用它。

这就是我们本周二的每周前 7 名！ 本周请关注 dev.to 的每日内容和讨论……并且一定要在未来关注这个系列。 你可能就在其中！



原文:

Every Tuesday we round up the previous week's top posts based on traffic, engagement, and a hint of editorial curation. The typical week starts on Monday and ends on Sunday, but don't worry, we take into account posts that are published later in the week.

7 DEVs Writing Quality Content About Your #Career

@jmfayard recently shouted out 7 inspiring #career writers that you definitely need to check out. This kind of list could never really be complete, so add your favorite DEV writers in the comments of this amazing post!

Top 20 Must-Know Tips for Web Accessibility

This awesome post by @ismaestro aims to provide you with essential principles, practical advice, and examples for creating accessible websites. You’ll learn to create clear descriptions for images, explore the world of navigating using only the keyboard, and much more, so that everyone can browse your site like a pro!

Please Don't Write Confusing Conditionals

Here, we’ll discuss with @somedood how confusing conditionals manifest in our code. Along the way, we also explore different refactoring techniques for improving the readability of conditional control flows.

The Magic of Empty Git Commit

Although seemingly counterintuitive, empty commits can offer valuable benefits for documentation, triggering automation. @pradumnasaraf demonstrates how to create and push an empty commit using Git.

A Comprehensive Beginner's Guide to NPM: Simplifying Package Management

This fully beginner-friendly guide from @abhixsh will take you through the fundamentals of NPM, providing you with a solid foundation for simplifying package management and streamlining your development process.

Real DOM, Virtual DOM, Shadow DOM, What's the Difference?

It can be hard to keep your head straight between real DOMs and virtual DOMs and shadow DOMs, and how they all work together to create a clean, performant Document Object Model. Let’s dive in with @lyndsiwilliams!

Introduction to CSS Grid: A Comprehensive Guide

CSS Grid is a layout system that allows you to create complex, grid-based designs with ease. Here, we'll explore the key concepts of CSS Grid with @joanayebola and demonstrate how to use it with practical code examples.

That's it for our weekly Top 7 for this Tuesday! Keep an eye on dev.to this week for daily content and discussions...and be sure to keep an eye on this series in the future. You might just be in it!
item.author: devencourt

item.title: PublishWise：撰写和发布博客的唯一场所 | PublishWise: The one place to write and publish your blogs
item.description:
它包括一个理想的编辑器，用于编写博客并将其发布到Dev.to和 HashNode（未来还会有更多）。 您可以通过在 PublishWise 上修改一次，轻松更新已在Dev.to和 HashNode 中发布的博客。 它简化了交叉发布并将您的博客整合到一个平台上。 这个项目有很大的潜力，未来将继续添加更多有价值的功能。

类别提交：

  • 最佳整体项目

  • 最赏心悦目

  • 最令人印象深刻的技术

  • 使用 [Supabase 构建的最佳项目

    应用链接

    该项目已在PublishWise.ink上线

    截图

    描述

    该项目是使用Refine启动的。 它使用Supabase作为数据库、存储和身份验证提供程序。 除此之外，它使用 Refine 数据挂钩在整个应用程序中执行查询和突变。 整个应用程序构建在 Next.js 应用程序目录之上，并利用 Next.js 的新功能。

    链接到源代码

    这个应用程序的源代码可以在GitHub上找到

    许可许可

    背景（是什么让您决定构建这个特定的应用程序？是什么启发了您？）

    我发布博客已经有一段时间了。 我通常在多个平台上发布它们，这意味着我会在Dev.to 、HashNode、Medium 和 Showcase 上交叉发布它们。 我发现这个过程极其重复、繁重且耗时。 到了更新的时候，就更重复、更累了。 于是我的脑海里浮现出一个想法。 为什么不构建一个应用程序来让我撰写文章并立即在所有这些平台上发布它们呢？ 这就是我构建PublishWise 的目的。 它不仅允许您撰写和发布博客，还可以从一个平台对其进行编辑，并且您的编辑将反映在您的所有博客中。 它将解决我自己以及成千上万其他技术作家的问题。 我对该应用程序有更大的计划，并希望为其添加更多有用的功能，以便它可以为用户增加更多价值，我将在我的 Twitter 帐户 ( @developeratul ) 上公开这样做。

    我是如何构建它的（你是如何利用精炼的？你一路上学到了新东西吗？掌握了新技能？）

    我在构建 PublishWise 时学到了很多东西。 我研究了我最喜欢的博客平台的 API。 我更加熟悉了新的 Next.js 应用程序目录和 React 服务器组件。 我接触到了令人惊叹的工具/库/服务，这将使我的开发工作变得更加轻松。 例如 Refine、Supabase、Mantine UI 和 Tiptap。

    该项目是使用Refine启动的。 它使用Supabase作为数据库、存储和身份验证提供程序。 除此之外，它使用 Refine 数据挂钩在整个应用程序中执行查询和突变。 整个应用程序构建在 Next.js 应用程序目录之上，并利用 Next.js 的新功能。

    其他资源/信息

    • 细化文档

    • 该博客是通过PublishWise 撰写和发布的 😉

萨达尔·穆达萨尔·阿里·汗 - 6 月 13 日

一旦被暂停，developeratul 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，developeratul 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，developeratul 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果developeratul没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记developeratul：

使所有由developeratul发布的帖子变得不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

不加标记的developatul将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

PublishWise is a tool that allows you to write and publish blogs from a single platform. It includes an ideal editor for writing blogs and publishing them on Dev.to and HashNode (with more to come). You can easily make updates to your blog that has been published in both Dev.to and HashNode by modifying it once on PublishWise. It simplifies cross-posting and consolidates your blogs onto a single platform. This project has a lot of potential and will continue to add more valuable features in the future.

Category Submission:

  • Best Overall Project

  • Most Visually Pleasing

  • Most Technical Impressive

  • Best Project built using [Supabase

    ](supabase.com/) as the main data provider for the refine app.

App Link

The project is live at PublishWise.ink

Screenshots

Description

This project was bootstrapped using Refine. It uses Supabase as the database, storage, and auth provider. The UI is built using the Mantine UI library together with Refine. Other than that, it uses Refine data hooks for performing queries and mutations throughout the whole application. The whole application is built on top of the Next.js app directory and utilizes the new features of Next.js.

Link to Source Code

The source of this app is available on GitHub

Permissive License

Apache-2.0 license

Background (What made you decide to build this particular app? What inspired you?)

I have been publishing blogs for a while now. I usually publish them on multiple platforms meaning I cross-post them on Dev.to, HashNode, Medium, and Showcase. I find that process extremely repetitive, overwhelming, and time-consuming. When it comes to updating them, it's even more repetitive and exhausting. So an idea popped into my mind. Why not build an app that would let me write my article and publish them on all of these platforms at once? That was my purpose behind building PublishWise. It not only allows you to write and publish your blogs but also edit them from one platform and your edit will be reflected in all of your blogs. It is going to solve my own problem plus thousands of other technical writers out there. I have bigger plans for that app and would like to add more useful features to it so it can add more value to the users and I will be doing that in public on my Twitter account (@developeratul).

How I built it (How did you utilize refine? Did you learn something new along the way? Pick up a new skill?)

I learned a lot while building PublishWise. I studied the apis of my favorite blogging platforms. I got more familiar with the new Next.js app directory and React server components. I got exposure to amazing tools/libraries/services that would make my development job so much easier. Such as Refine, Supabase, Mantine UI, and Tiptap.

This project was bootstrapped using Refine. It uses Supabase as the database, storage, and auth provider. The UI is built using the Mantine UI library together with Refine. Other than that, it uses Refine data hooks for performing queries and mutations throughout the whole application. The whole application is built on top of the Next.js app directory and utilizes the new features of Next.js.

Additional Resources/Info

  • Refine Docs

  • Refine-Supabase Data Provider

  • Mantine UI Docs

  • Supabase Docs

This blog was written and published through PublishWise 😉

Sardar Mudassar Ali Khan - Jun 13

Once suspended, developeratul will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, developeratul will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by developeratul will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If developeratul is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag developeratul:

Make all posts by developeratul less visible

developeratul consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging developeratul will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 在 Product Hunt 上推出 Dyte 的直播 SDK | Launching Dyte’s Live streaming SDK on Product Hunt
item.description:
里希特·班萨尔

发表于 7 月 18 日

对于那些第一次听说 Dyte 的人，这里有一些简短的背景信息 -

我们早在 2020 年疫情期间就开始构建 Dyte。 最初是作为另一个视频通话平台开始的，在收到多个同样的请求后，我们很快转向提供视频和语音 SDK。 从那时起，这就是一段相当长的旅程🚀

我们非常高兴地宣布推出“Dyte 直播 SDK”，这是我们提供的解决方案套件的最新成员 🎉

作为一家多功能 SDK 提供商，将我们的产品扩展到包括直播是一个自然的进展。 直播可满足各种用例，特别是对于已经在利用我们的视频服务的公司。 我们认识到这一点，并将扩展我们的产品以满足尊贵用户的需求作为首要任务。 不用说，他们中的许多人已经成功整合了我们的直播 SDK，并受益于其卓越的性能。 经过数月的精心测试和优化，我们很高兴地宣布，我们的直播 SDK 已结束测试阶段，可供公众无缝集成和使用！

让我们深入研究一下我们的直播 SDK 提供的一些功能：

主要特征：

  1. 低延迟流媒体：体验闪电般快速的流媒体，延迟低至 2 秒，确保您的观众随时随地享受实时内容。
  2. 自定义布局：使用 Dyte 的 UI 套件制作视觉上引人入胜的视频源，使您能够为观众创建量身定制的、引人入胜的用户体验。
  3. 实时分析：全面了解直播的运行状况、用户活动和用户行为，为您提供有价值的数据以进行优化。
  4. 自适应比特率：无论网络条件如何，都可以享受流畅、不间断的直播，无需任何手动干预。
  5. 内置交互性：通过白板、聊天、反应、屏幕共享、投票和测验等功能促进参与和协作，使您的直播具有互动性和吸引力。
  6. 无缝添加主播：让观众轻松加入直播并与主播一起参与游戏或活动，增强整体体验。
  7. 可扩展性：借助 Dyte 强大的基础设施，可以轻松扩展流媒体以支持超过 10,000 名观众，同时保持卓越的视频质量。
  8. 平台无关：从任何设备和浏览器流式传输实时内容，确保各种视频编解码器的兼容性。 顺利开始！

我们今天正在进行“产品搜寻”活动！ 立即查看我们： www.producthunt.com/posts/live-streamin...

谢谢您，我期待您的意见和反馈！ 易卜拉欣·埃莫纳·阿卜杜拉希德 - 7 月 17 日

雅库布·安杰耶夫斯基 - 7 月 17 日

一旦被暂停，rishit 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被取消。

一旦解除暂停，rishit 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，rishit 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 rishit 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以采取以下措施来标记 rishit：

使 rishit 的所有帖子变得不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

坚持不懈的 rishit 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Rishit Bansal

Posted on Jul 18

 

For those of you who are hearing about Dyte for the first time, here’s some quick context -

We started building Dyte back in 2020 in the middle of the pandemic. Having started initially as yet another video calling platform we soon transitioned to offering video and voice SDKs after receiving several requests for the same. And it has been quite a journey ever since 🚀

We’re super excited to announce the launch of “Dyte’s live streaming SDK”, newest addition to the suite of solutions we provide 🎉

Dyte’s Live streaming SDK:

Dyte's Live-streaming SDK is designed to enhance your audio and video capabilities by providing a comprehensive range of features. As a versatile SDK provider, expanding our offerings to include live-streaming was a natural progression. Live-streaming caters to a variety of use-cases, particularly for companies already leveraging our video services. We recognized this and made it a priority to expand our offerings to meet the demands of our valued users. Needless to say, many of them have already successfully incorporated our live-streaming SDK, benefiting from its exceptional performance. After months of meticulous testing and optimization, we are thrilled to announce that our live-streaming SDK is out of beta and available to the public for seamless integration and utilisation!

Let's delve into some of the features that our Live-streaming SDK offers:

Key Features:

  1. Low latency streaming: Experience lightning-fast streams with latency as low as 2 seconds, ensuring your audience enjoys real-time content from anywhere.
  2. Custom layouts: Craft a visually captivating video feed with Dyte's UI Kits, allowing you to create a tailored and engaging user experience for your audience.
  3. Live analytics: Gain comprehensive insights into your live stream's health, user activity, and user behaviour, empowering you with valuable data for optimization.
  4. Adaptive bitrate: Enjoy smooth and uninterrupted live streams, regardless of network conditions, without requiring any manual intervention.
  5. Built-in interactivity: Foster engagement and collaboration with features like whiteboard, chat, reactions, screenshare, polls, and quizzes, making your live streams interactive and captivating.
  6. Seamless host addition: Enable viewers to effortlessly join the livestream and participate in games or activities with the streamer, enhancing the overall experience.
  7. Scalability: With Dyte's robust infrastructure, effortlessly scale your streams to support over 10,000 viewers while maintaining exceptional video quality.
  8. Platform agnostic: Stream live content from any device and browser, ensuring compatibility across a wide range of video codecs. Get started without a hitch!

We are live on Product Hunt today! Check us out right now: www.producthunt.com/posts/live-streamin…

Thank you and I look forward to your comments and feedback! 🙇

Ibrahim Enemona Abdulrasheed - Jul 17

Jakub Andrzejewski - Jul 17

item.title: 尝试为大型开源项目做出第一次贡献 - 一步一步 🛠️🚀 | Attempting Your First Contribution to a Major Open Source Project - Step by Step 🛠️🚀
item.description:
太长了；博士

  1. 查找项目文档中需要改进的地方（错误、语法、措辞、简洁性等）
  2. 添加您的更改+保存它们
  3. 确保存储库与主存储库同步- 如果不同步，请使用 git stash
  4. 创建您的 Pull 请求 (PR) = 将您的更改更新到主 React 存储库
  5. 给自己喝杯冰镇啤酒，等待维护人员审查你的 PR 🍻

嘿朋友们👋，

当您偶然发现一个很酷的开源项目并想：“啊，我希望我能做出贡献，但我还没有那么好......”时，您是否曾感到兴奋不已？

好吧，我今天的工作就是试图证明你错了……我想我可能会成功（抱歉但不抱歉💚）。

如果您不知道，当开发人员尝试为项目文档做出贡献时，通常就会为开源做出贡献。

这是了解更多有关该项目并教您为主要存储库做出贡献背后的所有高级机制的完美方式。 确实，一开始正确使用 GitHub 是一件相当可怕的事情。

现在，在本文中，我将向您介绍两件关键的事情：

  1. 一种渐进且系统的开源贡献方法。
  2. 有效贡献文档所需的知识和心态。

完成此操作后，您可以访问quine.sh ，它可以帮助您发现可以贡献的开源项目。 它根据您喜欢的主题和编程语言匹配项目（这里有一个 简短的播放列表，可让您了解如何使用它）。 它的使用是完全免费的。 现在，让我们逐步了解如何让您从“从未为开源文档做出过贡献”到“我刚刚记录了世界上最大的开源项目之一！”。

注意：您的贡献需要得到项目维护者的批准。 您将等待维护者的答复，他们可以接受、拒绝或要求更改您的贡献。 今天，您将提交对 React 文档的贡献- 这是 Meta 和最著名的前端 JavaScript 库做出的重要开源贡献之一。

但是，您可以选择您喜欢的任何项目并按照以下步骤操作（如果您喜欢机器学习，我会推荐 Pytorch） 。 您在React 文档中看到的所有内容都写在GitHub上的文件中，这说明了开源的要点。 您可以通过将更改上传到 GitHub 文件来更改官方网站上显示的内容 - 我发现这非常令人兴奋！ 刚开始你是不是有点紧张？ 别担心，我有你。 要求：如果这是您第一次在开源上做出贡献，您必须阅读并执行本文中所示的步骤（或者，您可以观看Youtube 视频）。 这将只需要您 10 分钟。 警告⛔️： Git 是您进行开源贡献的重要工具，它通过终端将您的本地计算机链接到 GitHub。 与 Git 发生冲突是很正常的！ 如果按照步骤操作时出现问题，请随时咨询 Google、ChatGPT 或 StackOverflow。 请记住，几乎每个程序员都会遇到 Git 挑战。 对于我们中 95% 更喜欢视频而不是文本的人来说，请找到下面的视频来帮助您做出贡献！ 这可能是语法上的改进，重写一些不清楚的内容或需要简洁的地方。

就我而言，我认为圈出的句子需要简洁一些。

我想将其缩短为：

复制要更改的 React 文档句子/段落并将其粘贴到 GitHub 的搜索栏中。 选择选项Search in this repository 。

此时，您已经找到了源代码所在的确切文件 - 记下它以供稍后使用。

现在您的本地计算机上已拥有 React 的本地副本 💻

这是非常重要的一步，因为您有时会了解每个存储库在贡献时所具有的特定规则。 注意： React 的 README 页面非常适合详细介绍贡献，并且可以用于将来的贡献。 就我而言，它是src/content/learn/index.md

此时，您将看到您已做出哪些更改并且应该准备好提交！

如果您愿意，可以直接跳到步骤 6 。 但是，我想确保您可以处理一些边缘情况，例如当您进行更改时 GitHub 上的原始 React 存储库发生更改。 您即将将本地计算机中的内容更新为在线分叉 GitHub 存储库中的内容 - yayyyy！

然而，正确的做法是在线访问您的 GitHub 存储库并确保您拥有最新版本的 React 官方存储库。 这是因为有时，当您工作时，主存储库可能会发生一些更改，因此确保您拥有最新版本符合您的最佳利益。

要做到这一点，很简单：前往您的在线存储库并单击“ Sync fork ”。 如果您落后于某些更改，GitHub 会自动将您的版本更新到最新版本。

如果您的存储库不同步，因为在您进行更改的同时有人更改了 React 的存储库，您需要执行以下步骤。

要继续，您需要了解git pull的概念。 当您在终端中运行git pull命令时，您会检查其他人是否更改了您正在处理的文件的任何部分。 如果原始存储库已更改，您可以使用 git pull 确保将最新更新“拉取”到您的计算机中。 这使您可以使代码与远程存储库中的最新更改保持同步。

换句话说，运行git pull是一种检查其他人是否更改了相同代码行并将这些更改引入您的项目版本的方法。

现在轮到你了：🪄

在这里你会遇到一个错误，因为你知道你当前的版本落后于最新版本的 React。

注意：如果对其他文件进行了更改，则不会出现错误，并且无需 git stash 即可继续（并且可以直接跳到步骤 6）。

这将暂时保存您对工作目录所做的更改，而无需提交它们。 它们将暂时隐藏，同时允许您从在线 GitHub 存储库获取文件的版本。

现在，这会将最新版本从您的在线 GitHub 存储库拉取到您的本地计算机中。

您现在应该在 IDE 中看到这些更改。 删除您不需要的更改并保留您想要的更改。

运行它始终是一个很好的做法，因为您基本上可以看到“发生了什么”。 这会将您所做的所有更改添加到 Git 上。

如果这是您第一次推送更改，系统会要求您验证自己的身份。 我建议您阅读我之前的文章中的步骤 4.4-4.8。

好吧，我们现在已经很接近了！ 最后一搏🪄

我在下面附上一张图片，以便您可以看到它应该是什么样子：

之后，您所需要做的就是等待。 正在发生的事情是 React 的项目所有者（也称为维护者）需要接受您的拉取请求（也称为合并拉取请求）。

根据您的贡献，您可以接受、拒绝它，或者维护人员可以要求进行一些调整。

这完全取决于您所提供的贡献类型，因此只要您添加/修改的内容有意义，您的贡献就应该被接受！ 就是这样的人！

总而言之，你应该为自己感到骄傲。 这是相当技术性的，尤其是在学习 GitHub 机制方面。 然而，你做得非常出色。 因此，坐下来放松一下，因为您刚刚完成了所需的一切！ 我们知道第一次总是非常艰难，而且你能做到这一点真的很酷 - 为你感到骄傲！ 您现在已经了解了整个 GitHub 贡献流程背后的技术细节以及贡献方法！

如果您准备好开始为其他项目做出贡献，我们已经编制了一份项目列表，其中包含您可以开始的简单问题。 查看quine.sh上的项目列表，这里有一个 简短的播放列表，以防您被阻止。 如果您还没有，如果您需要任何帮助或有任何疑问，可以加入我们的不和谐服务器。 🫶 🫶



原文:

tl;dr

  1. Find areas of improvement in a project's documentation (errors, grammar, wording, conciseness etc.)
  2. Fork + Clone the React repo (short for repository) = Copy repo onto your own GitHub + download it on your local computer
  3. Add Your Changes + Save them
  4. Make sure repos are in sync with the main repos - if not, use git stash
  5. Git push = Upload your changes to your GH account
  6. Create Your Pull Request (PR) = Update your changes to the Main React Repo
  7. Get yourself a nice cold beer and wait for maintainers to review your PR 🍻

Hey friends 👋,

Did you ever felt that twinge of excitement when you stumbled upon a cool open-source project and thought, “Ahhh I wish I could contribute but I’m just not that good yet…” 🤕

Well, my job today will be to try to prove you wrong… and I think I might succeed (sorry but not sorry 💚).

In case you didn’t know, a solid start to contributing to open source usually happens when developers attempt to contribute to the project’s documentation.

This is the perfect way to understand more about the project and teaches you all the advanced mechanics behind contributing to a major repository. Indeed, using GitHub correctly is quite a scary thing at first.

Now, in this article, I'll equip you with two key things:

  1. A gradual and systematic approach to open source contributions.
  2. The knowledge and mindset required to contribute to documentation effectively.

After completing this, you can visit quine.sh, which helps you discover open-source projects to contribute to. It matches projects based on your preferred topics and programming languages (here's a short playlist to let you know how to use it). The use of it is completely free. 🫶

Now, let’s look into all the step-by-step to get you from “never contributed to documentation in open source” to “I just documented one of the biggest open source projects in the world!”.

Note: Your contributions are subject to the approval of the project's maintainer(s). You will wait for the answer from the maintainer(s) who could either accept, decline or ask for changes to your contributions. 😀

Today, you will submit your contribution to React’s documentation - one of the essential open-source contributions made by Meta and the most famous front-end JavaScript library.

However, you can pick any project of your liking and follow the below steps (I would recommend Pytorch if you like Machine Learning). 🙃🪄 

Everything you see on the React Documentation is written in a file on GitHub, which illustrates the point of open source. You can change what appears on the official website by uploading changes to a GitHub file - I find that quite mind-blowing! 🤯

Are you a little bit nervous to get started? Don’t worry, I got you. 🤝

Requirement: If this is the first time you have contributed to anything on open source, you must read and do the steps shown in this article (alternatively, you can watch the Youtube video). This will take you only 10 min. 🧢

WARNING ⛔️: Git is your essential tool for open-source contributions, linking your local computer to GitHub via your terminal. Struggling with Git is NORMAL! If issues arise when following the step-by-step, don't hesitate to consult Google, ChatGPT, or StackOverflow. Remember, virtually every coder encounters Git challenges. 🫶

For the 95% of us that prefer videos instead of text, find the below video to assist you in contributing! 🌟

1. Read the Documentation

1️⃣ Go to React Documentation.

2️⃣ Try to find areas that could be improved. This could be a grammatical improvement, a re-write of something unclear or areas that could use some conciseness.

In my case, I think the circled sentence could use some conciseness.

I want to shorten it to:

“React applications are built using components, which are self-contained UI segments varying in size from buttons to entire pages, each with its logic and styling.”

3️⃣ Head to React GitHub repository to find the original code file. Copy React’s documentation sentence/paragraph you want to change and paste it into GitHub’s search bar. Chose the option Search in this repository.

At this point, you have found the exact file where the source code is based - keep a note of it for later on.

2. Fork and Clone React Repo = Copy Repo onto my own GH and local computer

1️⃣ Head back to the main react page and fork the repository. Fork means you will create a copy of this repository in your GitHub account. 💪

2️⃣ Open your terminal and navigate to the folder (also called directory in technical terms) of your choice.

3️⃣ Copy the HTTPS link from the repo by clicking on the double square shown below.

4️⃣ Clone the repo by running git clone PASTE-YOUR-HTTPS-LINK-HERE inside your terminal. Now you have a local copy of React on your local computer 💻

3. Add Your Changes and Save Them

1️⃣  Before going further, read the “CONTRIBUTING.md” page guideline and the “Code Of Conduct. This is a super important step as you sometimes learn about specific rules that each repo has when contributing. Note: React’s README page is perfect for going through in details contributions and this can be used for future contributions.🫡

2️⃣ Now open the react project with your favourite coding environment.

3️⃣ Find the file you want to modify using the file path from step 1.4. In my case, it was src/content/learn/index.md

4️⃣ Add your changes and save them.

5️⃣ Review your changes by running on your terminal git diff. At this point, you will see what changes you have made and should be ready to commit!

You can jump straight into step 6 - if you want to. However, I would like to make sure you can handle some edge cases, such as when the original React repository on GitHub changed while you have made changes. 🤔

4. Check your Online GitHub Repo is the same as the main react repo

You are about to update what is in your local computer into what is on your online forked GitHub repo - yayyyy!

However, the right thing to do is head online to your GitHub repo and ensure you have the latest version of react official’s repo. This is because sometimes, while you are working, the main repository could have gotten some changes, so it is in your best interest to ensure you have the most up-to-date version.

To go about it, it is straightforward: head over to your online repo and click on “Sync fork”. If you are behind on some of the changes, GitHub will automatically update your version to the latest version.

5. If not in Sync → use Git Stash

If your repo was not in sync as someone changed the React’s repo in the meantime you were making your changes, you need to do the below steps.

To proceed, you need to understand the concept of git pull. 👇

When you run the git pull command in your terminal, you check if someone else has changed any part of the file you've been working on. If the original repository has changed, you make sure with git pull that you “pull the latest updates” into your computer. This lets you keep your code up-to-date with the latest changes from the remote repository.

In other words, running git pull is a way to check if someone else has changed the same lines of code and bring those changes into your version of the project.

Now your turn: 🪄

1️⃣ Run in your terminal git pull to ensure you are up-to-date with the repo. Here you will encounter an error since you know that your current version is behind the latest version of React.

Note: If the changes were made to other files, you won’t get an error and you can proceed without needing git stash (and you could jump straight into step 6).

2️⃣ Run git stash. This will temporarily save changes you have made to your working directory without committing them yet. They will be hidden for the time being while allowing you to get the version of the files from your online GitHub repository.

3️⃣ Run git pull again. This will now pull the latest version from your online GitHub repo into your local computer.

4️⃣ Run git stash pop. You should see now in your IDE the changes. Delete the changes you don’t want and keep the ones you want.

6. Git push = Upload your changes to your GH account

1️⃣ Run git status to display the state of the working directory and the staging area. It is always a good practice to run it as you essentially get to see “what’s happened”. 👀

2️⃣  Run git add . This adds all the changes you did onto Git.

3️⃣ Add a comment to your commit by running: git commit -m "grammatically updated the tech doc”.

4️⃣  Run git push. You will be asked to authenticate yourself if this is your first time pushing changes. I advise you to read my previous article from step 4.4-4.8.

5️⃣ You may receive an error, after which you should copy and run this: git push origin HEAD

7. Create Your Pull Request = Update your changes to the Main React Repo

Alright, we are so close to it now! One last push 🪄

1️⃣  Head back to your forked repository on GitHub.

2️⃣  Click on Contribute and then click on Compare and pull request.

3️⃣  Describe your commit message and why you made the changes - its always a good time to go back to the Contributing guidelines to check whether you have done everything properly. 🌟

4️⃣ Click on “Pull Request”! 🎉🍾👯‍♀️

5️⃣ Your pull request is now on its way! Meta will prompt you to sign their Contributor License Agreement. I attach a picture below so you can see what it should look like:

After this, all you need to do is wait. What is happening is React’s project owners (also called maintainers), need to accept your pull request (it is also called merging a pull request).

Depending on your contributions, you can get it accepted, declined or maintainers could ask for some adjustments.

This is entirely dependent on the type of contributions you have given so as long as what you added/modified makes sense, your contribution should get accepted! 🌟

That’s it people!

All in all, you should be so proud of yourself. This has been quite technical, especially with learning the GitHub mechanics. However, you did it splendidly. 💃 

So sit back and relax as you have just done everything you needed! ☀️🧘‍♂️🎉

We know it is always super tough the first time, and it's really cool you got to this point - so proud of you! 👯‍♀️💚

You have now learned the technicalities behind the entire GitHub contribution process and the methodology for contributing!

If you are ready to start contributing to other projects, we have compiled a list of projects with easy issues you can start on. Check out the list of projects on quine.sh, and here's a short playlist in case you are blocked. 🚀

If you haven't yet, you could join our discord server if you need any help or have any questions. 🫶
item.author: fernandezbaptiste

item.title: 副机长下一个免费副驾驶 | Cocaptain the next free Copilot
item.description:
阿尤布-ESSADEQ

发表于 7 月 18 日

  • 主要特征：

    1. 互动聊天：直接从编辑器中与 ChatGPT 进行互动对话。 获得即时响应并接收有关代码、文档等的智能建议。

    2. 代码协助：利用 ChatGPT 的强大功能来协助您处理与代码相关的查询。 询问有关特定编程语言、库、框架或编码概念的问题，并获得根据您的需求量身定制的有用答复。

    3. 上下文代码生成：根据您与 ChatGPT 的对话生成代码片段、函数原型和样板代码。 快速构建您的项目并简化您的开发工作流程。

    4. 代码执行：您可以直接从聊天面板执行 chatgpt 建议的代码。

    5. 全新的开发体验：将chatgpt引入vscode的强大之处在于，chatgpt将能够获得vscode的强大功能，允许您创建命令来生成数据库、目录、服务器、安装依赖项、代码生成..

    6. 安全和私密：该扩展优先考虑隐私和数据安全。 我们扩展的强大之处在于它不依赖除 chatgpt webapp 之外的任何第三方，这意味着您实际上是在 vscode 中使用 chatgpt。

    指示

    • 要使用 vscode 命令为您与 chatgpt 进行的对话分配标题： assign title to conversation并输入您要分配的标题。
    • 如果您想与 chatgpt 开始新对话，请使用 vscode 扩展： new conversation ，然后您必须开始在新上下文中询问 chatgpt。
    • 要从活动文本编辑器询问 chatgpt，请遵循以下语法： OpeningComment + ask + question + ? +closingComent 它会自动检测您当前使用的编程语言。 这是一个例子：
    • 如果您想创建一个目录但没有时间这样做，请创建一个新的 powershell 文件，然后编写如下内容：

    • 如果要安装许多依赖项，请在 powershell 中执行相同的操作。
    • 在活动文本编辑器中使用 chatgpt 时，请确保要求他仅将脚本引入一个代码块中。
    • 如果您想直接从聊天面板运行脚本，则应确保满足所需的依赖项和要求（例如，要运行 javascript，您需要安装 Node js）。
    • 要清除对话，您只需删除 chatGPT.json 文件的内容即可。

萨西达尔·加德帕利 - 5 月 15 日

一旦被暂停，ayyoubessadeq 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被取消。

一旦解除暂停，ayyoubessadeq 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，ayyoubessadeq 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 ayyoubessadeq 没有​​被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 ayyoubessadeq：

降低 ayyoubessadeq 的所有帖子的可见度

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 ayyoubessadeq 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Ayyoub-ESSADEQ

Posted on Jul 18

 
  • Cocaptain extension allows you to connect to ChatGPT directly from within your Visual Studio Code editor, enabling you to leverage its advanced natural language processing capabilities without leaving your coding environment.

Key Features:

  1. Interactive Chat : Engage in interactive conversations with ChatGPT right from your editor. Get instant responses and receive intelligent suggestions for code, documentation, and more.

  2. Code Assistance : Harness the power of ChatGPT to assist you with code-related queries. Ask questions about specific programming languages, libraries, frameworks, or coding concepts, and receive helpful responses tailored to your needs.

  3. Contextual Code Generation : Generate code snippets, function prototypes, and boilerplate code based on your conversation with ChatGPT. Quickly scaffold your projects and streamline your development workflow.

  4. Code execution : you could execute the code suggested by chatgpt right from the chatpanel.

  5. Whole New Developement experience : the power of bringing chatgpt to vscode is that chatgpt will have access to the power of vscode, allowing you to create command to generate databases, directories, servers, installing dependencies, code generation ..

  6. Secure and Private : The extension prioritizes privacy and data security. The power of our extension is it doesn't rely on any third party beside chatgpt webapp, which means that you are literally using chatgpt in vscode.

Instructions

  • To assign a title to the coversation you having with chatgpt using the vscode command : assign title to conversation and enter the title you want to assign.
  • If you want to start a new conversation with chatgpt use vscode extension : new conversation and then you have to start asking chatgpt in the new context.
  • To ask chatgpt from the active text editor respect this syntax : OpeningComment + ask + question + ? +closingComent then click tab. It autodetects the programming language you currently using. Here is an example :
  • If you want to create a directory and you don't have time to do so, create a new powershell file, and there write something like :

  • If there many dependencies that you want to install, do the same in powershell.
  • When using chatgpt in the active text editor make sure to ask he to bring only the script in one codeblock.
  • If you want to run a script right from the chat panel, you should make sure that the required dependencies and requirement are met (for example to run javascript you need to have Node js installed).
  • To clear the conversation you just delete the content of chatGPT.json file.

sasidhar Gadepalli - May 15

Once suspended, ayyoubessadeq will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ayyoubessadeq will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ayyoubessadeq will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ayyoubessadeq is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag ayyoubessadeq:

Make all posts by ayyoubessadeq less visible

ayyoubessadeq consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging ayyoubessadeq will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 在欧盟、英国和瑞士接受 PayPal 作为 Stripe 的付款方式 | Accept PayPal as a payment method with Stripe in the EU, UK and Switzerland
item.description:
我们最近在欧盟、英国和瑞士推出了PayPal作为付款方式，因此您现在可以为您的客户提供另一种使用其 PayPal 帐户提交付款的方式。
如果您有Connect 帐户，请首先联系支持团队，看看您是否符合资格。 如果您是直接商家，即您直接接受消费者的付款，让我们完成启用它的步骤。

要求

此功能仅适用于欧洲经济区（匈牙利除外）、英国和瑞士设置的 Stripe 账户。 如果您的 Stripe 账户是在其他国家/地区注册的，那么您目前将无法激活 PayPal。
您还需要在欧洲经济区、瑞士或英国拥有 PayPal 企业帐户。 您可以在入职过程中创建一个。

启用贝宝

要在Stripe Dashboard中启用 PayPal 功能，请导航至“付款设置”页面。 如果您符合资格，您应该会在“钱包”部分下看到“PayPal”选项。

单击“打开”按钮开始入职流程。

确认您的帐户满足所有要求，然后单击“继续”。

然后，选择您想要保存 PayPal 资金的位置，然后单击“连接到 PayPal”按钮以连接并激活您的帐户。

或者，您可以选择将资金存入您的 PayPal 账户：

当您被重定向到 PayPal 时，您可以登录现有帐户或创建一个新帐户作为该过程的一部分。 如上一节所述，您的 PayPal 帐户需要是欧洲经济区、瑞士或英国的企业帐户。

完成此步骤后，如果您登录现有帐户，您应该会在付款方式右侧看到一个绿色的复选标记，表明您的 PayPal 帐户已成功连接到 Stripe。

从那里，您可以启用或禁用定期付款，如果您需要更改资金结算方式，也可以联系支持人员。

如果您在入职流程中创建了新帐户，则该帐户将首先等待批准，直到您验证电子邮件为止。

您可以随时关闭此付款方式，方法是打开详细信息下拉列表并单击 Stripe 仪表板中的“关闭”按钮。

如果您主要使用 Stripe 的无代码产品（例如Payment Links ） ，那么这就是您需要做的。 整合贝宝

无代码解决方案

在您的帐户上启用 PayPal 后，您可以通过以下方式测试该功能是否正常工作：导航到您的产品页面，选择您为其创建付款链接的产品，单击定价部分下的“查看付款链接”按钮，复制链接，然后在浏览器中打开它。 您应该会看到 PayPal 显示为付款方式。

然后，您的客户将被引导通过 PayPal 的 UI 来确认付款。

结帐集成

使用 Stripe API 将 PayPal 集成为支付方式只需对代码进行最少的更改。

首先，如果您使用的是 Checkout，请在代码库中找到要创建 Checkout 会话的位置。 如果您已经使用payment_method_types属性指定不同的付款方式，则您所需要做的就是将paypal添加到数组中。

 const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.create({ mode: "payment", payment_method_types: ["card", "paypal"], line_items: [ { price: process.env.PRICE, quantity: 1, }, ], success_url: `${domainURL}/success.html?session_id={CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}`, cancel_url: `${domainURL}/canceled.html`, });

默认情况下，如果您不指定任何付款方式类型，则会显示所有启用的付款方式，包括 PayPal。

 const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.create({ mode: "payment", line_items: [ { price: process.env.PRICE, quantity: 1, }, ], success_url: `${domainURL}/success.html?session_id={CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}`, cancel_url: `${domainURL}/canceled.html`, });

支付元素集成

如果您使用Payment Element ，请找到您创建PaymentIntent位置。 如果您将属性automatic_payment_methods设置为启用，PayPal将自动显示为一个选项。 否则，如果您使用payment_method_types属性来自定义选项，则只需将paypal添加到数组中即可。

 const paymentIntent = await stripe.paymentIntents.create({ amount: 1999, currency: currency, payment_method_types: ['card', 'paypal'] });

上面显示的这些代码示例使用 Node.js，但其他编程语言中的更改非常相似。 如果您想了解更多信息，请查看文档。

退款

如果您需要向使用 PayPal 付款的客户退款，请导航至 Stripe 仪表板中的付款页面，选择您要为其退款的付款，单击退款按钮，然后在显示弹出窗口。

结论

如果您在欧盟、英国或瑞士注册了 Stripe 帐户，现在只需点击几下并进行一些小的代码更改，即可启用 PayPal 作为 Stripe 的付款方式。 此功能将使您能够接触更多潜在客户并为他们提供更好的结账体验。 如果您有兴趣了解更多信息，请查看我们的文档。

关于作者

她喜欢研究和试验技术。 当她不编码时，她喜欢在户外度过时光、阅读以及为自己设定随机挑战。



原文:

We recently made PayPal available as a payment method in the EU, UK, and Switzerland, so you can now offer an additional way for your customers to submit payments using their PayPal account.
If you have a Connect account, first reach out to the support team to see if you’re eligible. If you’re a direct merchant, meaning you accept payments directly from consumers, let’s go through the steps to enable it.

Requirements

This feature is only available for Stripe accounts set in the European Economic Area (with the exception of Hungary), the UK, and Switzerland. If your Stripe account is registered in another country, you will unfortunately not be able to activate PayPal at this time.
You’re also required to have a PayPal business account in the EEA, Switzerland or UK. You can create one during the onboarding process.

Enabling PayPal

To enable the PayPal feature in the Stripe Dashboard, navigate to the Payments settings page. If you’re eligible, you should see “PayPal” as an option under the “Wallets” section.

Click the “Turn on” button to start the onboarding process.

Confirm that your account meets all the requirements and click “Continue”.

Then, select where you’d like to keep your PayPal funds and click the “Connect to PayPal” button to connect and activate your accounts.

Alternatively you can choose to settle your funds into your PayPal account instead:

When you are redirected to PayPal, you can either log into an existing account or create a new one as part of the process. As mentioned in the previous section, your PayPal account needs to be a business account in the EEA, Switzerland or the UK.

Once this step is done, if you logged into an existing account, you should see a green checkmark on the right of the payment method to indicate that your PayPal account has been successfully connected to Stripe.

From there, you can enable or disable recurring payments as well as contact support if you need to change how your funds are settled.

If you created a new account as part of the onboarding flow, it will first be pending approval until you verify your email.

You can turn this payment method off at any time by opening the details dropdown and clicking the “Turn off” button in the Stripe Dashboard.

If you’re mostly using Stripe’s no-code products such as Payment Links, this is all you need to do. PayPal will now be a payment option for all your customers.

Integrating PayPal

No-code solution

Once you’ve enabled PayPal on your account, you can test that the feature is working by navigating to your products page, selecting a product you created a Payment Link for, click on the “View payment link” button under the Pricing section, copy the link, and open it in your browser. You should see PayPal displayed as a payment method.

Your customer will then be guided through PayPal’s UI to confirm the payment.

Checkout integration

Integrating PayPal as a payment method using the Stripe API requires minimal changes to your code.

First, if you’re using Checkout, locate where you are creating a Checkout session in your codebase. If you are already specifying different payment methods using the payment_method_types attribute, all you need to do is add paypal to your array.

 const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.create({ mode: "payment", payment_method_types: ["card", "paypal"], line_items: [ { price: process.env.PRICE, quantity: 1, }, ], success_url: `${domainURL}/success.html?session_id={CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}`, cancel_url: `${domainURL}/canceled.html`, });

By default, if you don’t specify any payment method types, it will display all payment methods enabled, including PayPal.

 const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.create({ mode: "payment", line_items: [ { price: process.env.PRICE, quantity: 1, }, ], success_url: `${domainURL}/success.html?session_id={CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}`, cancel_url: `${domainURL}/canceled.html`, });

Payment Element integration

If you’re using Payment Element, locate where you are creating the PaymentIntent. If you have the attribute automatic_payment_methods set to enabled, PayPal will show up as an option automatically. Otherwise, if you’re using the payment_method_types attribute to customize the options, you’ll only need to add paypal to the array and you’ll be good to go.

 const paymentIntent = await stripe.paymentIntents.create({ amount: 1999, currency: currency, payment_method_types: ['card', 'paypal'] });

These code samples shown above are using Node.js, but the change is very similar in other programming languages. If you want to read more, please check the docs.

Issuing refunds

If you need to issue a refund to a customer who paid using PayPal, navigate to the payments page in the Stripe Dashboard, select the payment you’d like to issue the refund for, click on the refund button, and confirm the refund in the pop-up displayed.

Conclusion

If you have a Stripe account registered in the EU, UK, or Switzerland, you can now enable PayPal as a payment method with Stripe with a few clicks and some minor code changes. This feature will allow you to reach more potential customers and provide them with a better checkout experience. If you’re interested in learning more, check out our docs.

About the author

Charlie Gerard is a developer advocate at Stripe, a published author and a creative technologist. She loves researching and experimenting with technologies. When she’s not coding, she enjoys spending time outdoors, reading, and setting random challenges for herself.
